commit.message:
feat: runtime get_post_metadata filter override for MPHB capacity GUI

Adds a priority-999 get_post_metadata filter after the class definition.
When MPHB requests _mphb_adults or _mphb_capacity, the filter intercepts
and returns purnukka_max_capacity from the DB instead, bypassing all of
MPHB's internal cache and read logic.

Critical fix vs user's proposed code: uses $wpdb->get_var() directly
instead of get_post_meta() — calling get_post_meta() inside a
get_post_metadata filter causes infinite recursion and a Fatal Error.

context.json bumped 2.0.5 → 2.0.6 to trigger provisioning on next load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: normalize LLM list fields to prevent Pydantic validation errors

instead of strings, causing PlanTask validation to fail. Added
normalize_string_list to coerce mixed types to strings.

commit.message:
fix(autopilot): read adAccountId from credential metadata, not encrypted data

Meta credentials store accessToken in encrypted data but adAccountId in
the metadata field. The activate endpoint was reading both from encrypted
data, causing "missing accessToken or adAccountId" error. Also strips
act_ prefix since meta-executor functions add it back.

commit.message:
Fix parallel tournament match execution (#964)

## Summary

- **Parallel match execution**: `tournament_game.py --games N` now runs
N matches truly in parallel via `ThreadPoolExecutor`. Added
`find_ready_matches()` to find all playable matches in the current round
(vs `find_next_match()` which returns only the first).
- **Fix concurrent build/startup races**: Pre-compiles once before
spawning parallel threads, then passes `--skip-compile` to orchestrator
subprocesses. This prevents 4 concurrent Maven builds fighting over the
same build directory and H2 lock file races where one process deletes
another's active lock.
- **Fix tournament game export race condition**: Tournament configs now
set `skipPostGamePrompts: True`. Export happens in `tournament_game.py`
after the bracket JSON is updated (instead of in the orchestrator, which
raced with the bracket write).
- **Fix server startup timeout in fresh worktrees**: Increased
`server_wait` from 90s to 240s (Maven classpath resolution takes 2+
minutes on first run). Added stale H2 lock file cleanup before server
start.
- **Game data**: Two Season 1 Round of 16 games exported with
leaderboard data regenerated.

## Test plan

- [x] `make check` passes (2 pre-existing failures from game exports
missing `youtubeUrl` — needs YouTube upload + re-export)
- [ ] Run `tournament_game.py --games 4` and verify matches start
simultaneously
- [ ] Verify `--skip-compile` flag works in orchestrator

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix preview (#21)

* Fix SwiftUI preview crashes from eager PairSetupHandler access

Property defaults and the hapSetupURI helper eagerly accessed
PairSetupHandler.setupCode/setupID, which hit preconditions because
keyStore is only set via PyloApp._ensureKeyStore — a path that
#Preview blocks bypass entirely. Move these reads into init (guarded
by skipRestore) and thread setupID through callers explicitly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add tests for preview factory to catch PairSetupHandler regressions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Validate setupID in hapSetupURI and set it in preview factory

Addresses PR review feedback: the preview factory never set setupID,
leaving it empty. Now the preview factory sets a placeholder ("PYLO"),
hapSetupURI validates setupID is exactly 4 ASCII characters, and tests
assert the expected format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add server-side rendered email card images via Satori

Replace inline HTML tables in premium email sections with pixel-perfect
PNG card images rendered server-side using next/og (Satori). Data is
base64url-encoded in the image URL at send time, so cards are frozen
snapshots that are infinitely cacheable (Cache-Control: immutable).

Six card types: traffic, citibike, airport, environmental, ferry, coat.
Apple Fitness Dark design language with score rings, progress bars, and
color-coded status indicators.

feat: mise integration for runtime resolution and auto-detection (#29)

* feat(mise): add RuntimeResolver with Available() and Which()

Introduce the internal/mise package with:
- Executor interface for testable mise CLI abstraction

commit.message:
Plaid Update Mode, smart sync feedback, balance anchor age display

PLAID UPDATE MODE (#1)
- PlaidClient.create_link_token() now accepts access_token param; when set,
  creates an update-mode link token (no products field) for in-place re-auth
- GET /api/plaid/update-link-token/{item_id}: create update-mode link token
  for a specific item using its stored (decrypted) access_token
- POST /api/plaid/update-complete/{item_id}: clears last_error_* fields and
  kicks off a background sync — no public_token exchange needed since the
  access_token is unchanged after update mode
- BankRow now has openReconnect() that opens Plaid Link in update mode;
  error banner includes a "🔗 Reconnect" button for ITEM_LOGIN_REQUIRED

SYNC FEEDBACK (#2)
- POST /api/plaid/sync-transactions now skips items with known errors instead
  of queuing them to fail again; response includes items_queued, items_errored,
  and per-item status list
- Global sync toast now differentiates: syncing N / skipped M (reconnect
  required) / all skipped (error-only toast)

BALANCE ANCHOR STALENESS (#3)
- /api/plaid/items accounts now include start_date, anchor_age_days,
  starting_balance so BankAccountRow can display anchor health
- BankAccountRow shows anchor date + age with color-coded staleness:
  neutral <90d, amber 90-180d, red >180d with "consider Force Resync" hint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: correction session shows original alongside rework; fix current.md accumulation

- AGENTS.md: new REWORKED block format includes <!-- INK:ORIGINAL:START/END -->
  so the author sees both reworked and original side-by-side in their editor
- book.rs: strip_engine_markers() now statefully removes ORIGINAL blocks
  (markers + content) before prose enters Full_Book.md
- book.rs: strip_author_ink_instructions() prevents echoed INK author
  comments from accumulating in current.md across sessions
- maintenance.rs: apply strip_author_ink_instructions() to engine prose
  before writing new current.md
- mcp_server.rs: init tool description updated — one question at a time,
  Config.yml fields updated in-place only, template structure preserved

commit.message:
refactor: complete retry architecture transition — remove escalation, unify issues CLI, replace circuit breaker (#214)

Phase 1: Remove EscalateToConflict and ConflictEscalator infrastructure.
         Rename permanent → actionable category for sync failures.
Phase 2: Consolidate schema — single migration, drop local_issues,
         remove dead remote_state columns, update CHECK constraints.
Phase 3: Unified `issues` CLI command (list/resolve/clear/retry) replacing
         separate conflicts, failures, and resolve commands. Hidden
         backward-compat aliases preserved.
Phase 4: Replace per-drive circuit breaker with account-wide throttle gate
         (429 Retry-After sets deadline, all workers wait).
Phase 5: Status command — Issues (conflicts + actionable) and Retrying
         (transient with failure_count >= 3) replace old counters.
Phase 6: Reconcile retry policy — MaxAttempts = 0 (infinite retries for
         transient failures).
Phase 7: Test and naming cleanup — rename stale references.
Phase 8: Documentation updates across CLAUDE.md, BACKLOG.md, LEARNINGS.md.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent bellows from marking PRs as ready-to-merge while reviews are pending (#127)

Bellows now fetches reviewRequests from the GitHub API alongside reviews
and tracks pending review requests in the PR state. PRs with outstanding
review requests (e.g., GitHub Copilot auto-reviews) are held until all
requested reviewers complete their review.

Changes:
- ghpr: add reviewRequests to gh pr view fields, ReviewRequest struct,
  and HasPendingReviewRequests() method
- state: add has_pending_reviews column to prs table with migration
- bellows: track HasPendingReviews in snapshot, include in mergeability
  update and restore-from-needs_fix condition
- IsPRReadyToMerge and ReadyToMergePRs queries now require
  has_pending_reviews = 0

commit.message:
Switch Qwen3-32B → Qwen3-14B for L4 QLoRA training

Qwen3-32B at 4-bit loads at 19.25GB on L4 (22GB), leaving insufficient
headroom for bitsandbytes dequantization buffers (~250MB per MLP layer)
plus activation memory during training. OOM confirmed at batch_size=1,
max_seq_len=256, packing=False with even 50 samples.

Qwen3-14B at 4-bit should load at ~8-9GB, leaving ~13GB for training.
Same hidden_size=5120 but 40 layers (vs 64), smaller MLP intermediate
(13696 vs 25600). Restore original training params: batch_size=4,
packing=True, reasonable seq lengths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: return 500 when yt-dlp produces no audio (bot check / unavailable)

Previously an empty segment returned 200 with 0 bytes and no X-More,
causing the client to silently treat it as track end. Now returns 500
so the client shows "Network error" instead.

Auto-approve Credit Line deposits using sourceBmId instead of API check

  (previously relied on Cheetah API call which could fail silently)

- Extension accounts: auto-approved, pending recharge via extension
